_comment_1: Generic-placeholder MCP config for qFoldIT/Protein-Design-MCP's 4 dependency-isolated environments. Replace every '/path/to/*_venv' with a real virtualenv/conda path on your machine before use.
_comment_2: WHY 4 SEPARATE ENVS: RFdiffusion pins torch==2.0.1, Boltz-2 needs torch>=2.2, ZairaChem is a conda-only AutoML stack (Mordred/GROVER/TabPFN/etc), and the quantum stack needs Qiskit + Amazon Braket SDK + qupepfold -- these pin conflicting/incompatible dependency versions and cannot coexist in one env. See pyproject.toml's [project.optional-dependencies] (gpu/boltz/zairachem/quantum) for exactly what to install in each.
_comment_3: CORRECTION vs. a naive 4-server design: this repo's server.py is a SINGLE FastMCP-style entrypoint that lazily imports each optional dependency only when its tools are actually called (see tools/__init__.py's lazy-import docstring) -- there are no separate boltz_server.py/quantum_server.py modules, and ZairaChem does not ship its own 'mcp-serve' subcommand (it's a plain CLI, invoked as a subprocess by this repo's own pipelines/zairachem_runner.py). All four entries below therefore point at the same 'protein_design_mcp.server' module; isolation comes entirely from which venv's site-packages each entry's VIRTUAL_ENV points at, not from four different Python modules.
